div.head:
No. 37.
Mr. Tree to Mr. Bayard.

opener.dateline:
Legation of the United States.
Brussels, June 30, 1888. (Received July 16.)

div.p: Sir: As the money coined and put in circulation by a new state has, perhaps, a certain interest, I have ventured to send you to-day by post, under a separate cover, specimens of the first moneys ever coined by the Independent State of the Congo.
The package contains specimens of pieces of a half franc, and of 1, 2, and 5 francs in silver, and of 1, 2, 5, and 10 centimes in copper. No gold pieces have been coined as yet.
